title: uragoga laxa
intro: Uragoga laxa, also known as the "Fever Tree", is a plant species that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. The medicinal properties of Uragoga laxa are derived from its bark, which contains various bioactive compounds such as saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide several health benefits including reduced fever, relief from pain and inflammation, and improved immune function. Uragoga laxa can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
